etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") + 攴(攴) (pū, "to tap") = symbolic act of signaling or authority -> original concept related to 修(修) (xiū, "to repair") -> later interpreted as ceremonial act involving water on bronzes indicating purification -> related to 滌(滌) (dí, "to wash")
etymology.interpretation: 攸 combines the elements of a person and a hand holding a stick, symbolizing authority or an action like a warning or purification. Originally associated with warning and repair, later interpretations on bronzes suggest a connection to ritualistic purification.
